title: Storyboard · "Knock" — Radio→Animation production script · S22
body:
**Scene 22: 27:30–28:30 · Wide of the City — Lit Windows**

HERO FRAME: Wide aerial of the city at night — but now NEARLY EVERY WINDOW is lit with warm amber light · streetlamps glow · a few figures (suggested, silhouetted) walking the streets together · a single church steeple's cross is lit on the horizon

MOTION: kling — windows light up one by one across the cityscape, a gentle wave from foreground to horizon. The streetlamps brighten. The cross on the steeple catches a beam of moonlight.

MOOD: Revelation 3:20 made visible.

AUDIO: Narrator (Walter, now in past tense, peaceful): *"I learned that the world had not been empty. I had been empty. The world had been waiting for me to answer the knock."*

source.label: Dimension X episode #05 (NBC Radio, May 6, 1950) adapting Fredric Brown's 1948 short-short story "Knock" (originally in *Thrilling Wonder Stories*, December 1948)

author: matt

extra.scene_title: 27:30–28:30 · Wide of the City — Lit Windows

witnesses.label: Rufinus of Aquileia, Commentary on the Apostles' Creed (c. AD 404)
Pirminius, De singulis libris canonicis scarapsus (c. AD 750) — earliest received form
Cited by every major catechism since 8th century
